name: Michigan State University Extension- Grand Rapids Kent County
description_short: MSU Extension provides free financial and housing education through certified counselors, webinars, and self-paced resources to Michigan residents seeking money management and homeownership guidance.
description_long:
Michigan State University Extension operates MI Money Health, a statewide educational program dedicated to improving financial literacy and housing outcomes for Michigan residents. Launched under MSU Extension's commitment to evidence-based community education, the program leverages university resources and certified professionals to deliver trusted financial guidance at no cost to consumers.

MI Money Health offers comprehensive educational services including foreclosure prevention counseling, homeownership seminars (both in-person and virtual), money management classes, self-paced online learning modules, and specialized presentations on emerging financial topics. Their curriculum covers fundamental topics like the home buying process, mortgage foreclosure options, tenant preparation through the Rent Smart program, retirement planning, and budgeting. The program employs certified housing and money management counselors who provide one-on-one expert consultation alongside group educational events and webinar sessions scheduled throughout the year.

What distinguishes MSU Extension is its university-backed credibility, non-commercial structure, and explicit mission to serve diverse audiences regardless of financial status. Rather than selling products or services, they position themselves as neutral educators demystifying financial concepts. Their approach includes innovative programming like the Smart Money Missions Goosechase Game targeting youth financial literacy, and specialized workshops addressing family dynamics around inheritance ("Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?"). Published impact reports from 2020-2021 document measurable outcomes of their educational interventions.

The primary limitation is that this is an educational resource rather than a direct service provider—they teach financial concepts and provide counseling but do not directly manage debt, process loans, repair credit, or provide emergency cash assistance. Geographic availability appears focused on Michigan residents, and while events are offered statewide, specific Grand Rapids/Kent County programming details are limited in available documentation. The program's effectiveness depends on consumer initiative to engage with offerings.
pros: Certified housing and money management counselors deliver professional guidance at no cost
Both online and in-person learning options accommodate different preferences and schedules
University-backed, non-profit structure ensures unbiased education without product sales incentives
Specialized programs address specific needs: foreclosure prevention, first-time homebuying, tenant preparation
Published impact reports (2020-2021) document measurable program outcomes and effectiveness
Free webinars and virtual classes reduce barriers to access for geographically dispersed consumers
Innovative youth-focused programs (Smart Money Missions) build financial literacy in younger generations
cons: Educational resource only—does not provide direct debt management, credit repair, or cash assistance services
Geographic limitation to Michigan residents may exclude out-of-state consumers
Relies on consumer self-direction and initiative; no proactive account management or ongoing support tracking
Limited specific information about Grand Rapids/Kent County service details or local counselor availability on main website
No emergency financial assistance for acute crises—strictly educational and advisory in scope
best_for: Michigan homebuyers seeking free education on mortgage processes and home purchase preparation
Homeowners facing foreclosure exploring options to keep, sell, or manage their properties
Young adults and families building foundational money management skills and financial literacy
Renters preparing for first apartment or lease with limited knowledge of tenant responsibilities
diagnosis: MSU Extension's MI Money Health is best for Michigan residents seeking free, credible financial and housing education delivered by university-backed certified counselors. The primary caveat is that this is purely educational and advisory—it provides no direct financial services, emergency cash, debt management, or credit repair, and requires consumers to proactively engage with offerings rather than providing managed account support.
services: Free financial and housing counseling from certified professionals
In-person homeownership education seminars at multiple Michigan locations
Virtual homeownership webinars covering the home buying process
Foreclosure prevention counseling and mortgage foreclosure options webinars
Money management classes and personal finance education
Self-paced online learning modules on financial topics
Rent Smart program covering financial preparation and tenant conduct
Family inheritance planning workshops ("Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?")
Smart Money Missions Goosechase Game for youth financial literacy (ages 13-19)
Custom community presentations addressing emerging financial issues
Articles, videos, publications, and learning resources on financial topics
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 775 Ball Ave., N.E., , Grand Rapids, MI 49503
